title: skill
description: Discoverable, on-demand procedural context, normally with SKILL.md (docs/ontology/README.md §1.1, core entity 'skill'). Non-equivalence rule from the same table: a skill is not a plugin and not necessarily a slash command. This file is the machine-readable concept declaration internal/ontology loads; it doubles as a JSON Schema for the canonical fields of one normalized skill record, plus x- extension keys carrying the logical-identity rule and allowed merge operators from docs/ontology/README.md §3.1/§3.2.

id.description: Stable logical skill ID (commonly the skill's directory/slug name).

name.description: The discoverable skill name used for same-name precedence (docs/ontology/README.md §6.1: 'Same-name: enterprise > personal > project > bundled').

description.description: Discovery metadata surfaced before the full skill loads (docs/architecture/reporting.md §8, 'Skill discovery metadata').

entrypoint.description: e.g. SKILL.md path relative to source.path.

x-nonEquivalenceRule: A skill is not a plugin and not necessarily a slash command.

x-logicalIdentity.rule: Two physical representations are the same logical skill only when both their name and discovery-source kind match. Same-named skills discovered under different source kinds (e.g. a project skill named 'code-review' versus a plugin-bundled skill also named 'code-review') compete as representations of one logical skill and are merged by precedence (docs/ontology/README.md §6.1: 'Same-name: enterprise > personal > project > bundled. Plugin skills are namespaced.'); they are not silently treated as unrelated entities just because their scope differs, and a name collision alone is not enough to declare identity across an entirely different source kind (a skill vs. a same-named plugin or slash command is a different entity, per the non-equivalence rule above).
